savage_verdict.roast: This command has more layers than a wedding cake and more complexity than quantum mechanics. 399 lines to switch context? My grandmother's recipe book is shorter and more practical. The ADHD optimization claims are bold, but this thing requires a PhD to understand. However, I grudgingly admit it's architecturally sound.

savage_conclusions.brutal_truth: Your command arsenal suffers from severe overengineering syndrome. 4 out of 5 commands exceed reasonable complexity levels. The average command is 359 lines - most functions should be under 20! You're building F-35 fighter jets when you need Honda Civics.
statistical_evidence: Mean complexity score 3.58/5 - dangerously close to CLAUDE.md 'STOP AND SIMPLIFY' threshold
Only 1/5 commands actually follows simplicity principles
Token consumption ranges from reasonable (7.7K) to disastrous (33.8K) - 4.4x variance
Success probability has σ=0.168 - that's gambling with production systems
Average 359 lines per command - 18x longer than recommended function length
actionable_brutality: DELETE ultrathink.md immediately - it's a token-burning complexity nightmare
Refactor adhd-context-switch.md to under 200 lines - current state is user-hostile
Study java-rapid-implementation.md - it's your only sane command
Apply 3-Question Rule to ALL commands - you're failing basic simplicity tests
Set hard limit: no command over 100 lines without mathematical proof of necessity
grudging_admissions: adaptive-complexity-router concept is brilliant despite execution bloat
Memory integration patterns are consistently well-designed

immediate_actions: Implement complexity budget: max 3.0 score per command
Enforce line limits: 100 lines typical, 200 lines maximum
Add success probability testing - aim for 80%+ minimum
Token efficiency targets: 10K tokens maximum per command
architectural_fixes: Extract common patterns into reusable components
learning_opportunities: Study why java-rapid-implementation succeeds - apply those patterns
Measure actual user completion rates vs. theoretical success probability
A/B test simplified versions against current bloated implementations
Implement command usage analytics to identify actual vs. theoretical value

final_savage_assessment.summary: Your command collection demonstrates impressive intellectual ambition married to catastrophic practical execution. You've built a thinking framework that thinks about thinking about thinking. Most commands violate basic simplicity principles while delivering marginally better outcomes than direct approaches. The one shining example (java-rapid-implementation) proves you CAN build simple, effective commands - you just usually choose not to.
final_savage_assessment.recommendation: Fire your current architect (you) and hire the person who wrote java-rapid-implementation (also you, but the sane version). Your commands need Marie Kondo, not more features.
final_savage_assessment.confidence_level: 95% - These numbers don't lie, and neither do I.
